title: Investigate Charges — NLRB
description: NLRB page describing how the agency investigates unfair-labor-practice (ULP) charges under Section 8 of the NLRA: charge filing, Board-agent investigation and affidavits, Regional Director merit determination (typically 7–14 weeks), settlement efforts, complaint issuance, Administrative Law Judge hearing, and appeal to the NLRB Office of Appeals — plus Section 10(j) injunctions and make-whole remedies.
notes: DEADLINE: a ULP charge must be filed within 6 months of the alleged violation (NLRA §10(b)); appeal of a dismissed charge to the Office of Appeals within 2 weeks of dismissal. The NLRB cannot assess monetary penalties; remedies are make-whole (reinstatement, backpay) and notice posting.
